name: pwm_controller
description: Parameterized PWM controller with firmware-accessible memory-mapped register interface. The top module integrates register access, duty/period configuration, free-running modulo counter behavior, and PWM output generation in a single synthesizable block.

fixed_assumptions: Synchronous active-low reset is sampled on clk.
Register reads are combinational or single-cycle internally but must return the live addressed register value.
Writes take effect on the active clock edge when wr_en is asserted.
Only a single clock domain is present.

functionality: Implements a firmware-programmable PWM generator with a memory-mapped register interface. CONTROL at 0x00 provides ENABLE in bit 0. DUTY_CYCLE at 0x04 stores an 8-bit duty threshold. PERIOD at 0x08 stores an 8-bit wrap value. COUNTER_VALUE at 0x0C is a read-only live status register reflecting the current counter state. While CONTROL.ENABLE is high and PERIOD is nonzero, the counter increments on each rising clk edge. When the counter reaches PERIOD, it wraps to zero on the next increment event. pwm_out is asserted when enabled and the current counter_value is strictly less than duty_cycle. Reads return the addressed register value, including live counter status, and writes only affect writable registers.
responsibilities: Decode wr_en, rd_en, wr_addr, and rd_addr into register accesses.
Store and update CONTROL.ENABLE, DUTY_CYCLE, and PERIOD.
Maintain the free-running counter according to ENABLE and PERIOD semantics.
Expose the live counter value on counter_value and through reads of 0x0C.
Generate pwm_out from ENABLE, counter_value, and duty_cycle.
Implement reset behavior for all registers and outputs.
Support deterministic readback of control and configuration registers.
reset_behavior: When reset_n is low, CONTROL.ENABLE, DUTY_CYCLE, PERIOD, COUNTER_VALUE, rd_data, pwm_out, and the externally visible counter_value output all reset to zero. No counter advancement or register updates occur while reset_n is low.
behavior_rules: CONTROL register is located at address 0x00; only bit 0 is functionally used and all other bits read as zero.
DUTY_CYCLE register is located at 0x04 and stores duty_cycle[7:0].
PERIOD register is located at 0x08 and stores period[7:0].
COUNTER_VALUE register is located at 0x0C, is read-only, and returns the live counter value.
Counter increments only when CONTROL.ENABLE is high and PERIOD is nonzero.
If counter_value equals PERIOD during an enabled increment event, the next counter value shall be zero rather than PERIOD+1.
pwm_out shall be high only when CONTROL.ENABLE is high and counter_value < duty_cycle.
Reads shall return the selected register value for the addressed location and zero for unmapped addresses.
Writes to read-only status locations shall have no effect.
counter_value output shall always mirror the current internal counter state.
The design shall remain synthesizable and use no latches.
All register updates shall be synchronous to clk.
